summary:
The study found higher extubation failures using conventional CPAP than bubble CPAP; thus, comparing their respective failure rates requires analyzing categorical data through an appropriate statistical test like the chi-squared analysis which evaluates differences in observed versus expected outcomes across groups. Agent1 reasoned by identifying the need for testing proportion comparison due to independence among sample patients under different treatment conditions, leading them to conclude the use of a Chi-square test was optimal based on its capability to handle such discrete event analyses efficiently. 

The summary has been kept within two concise sentences focusing only on critical aspects related to choosing the right statistical approach without extraneous details. Key elements include highlighting differing failure rates per group type and specifying why a specific hypothesis-testing methodology—Chi square—is chosen over others because it suits comparative studies involving countable events distributed categorically amongst separate populations being studied simultaneously. This aligns closely but succinctly captures core logic behind selecting this particular analytical tool relative to provided scenario specifics about medical intervention efficacy assessment via
